---
title: "No 3d acceleration"
date: 2008-07-18
forum: New to Ubuntu
---

### Post by wrtpeeps on 2008-07-18
Ever since I modified my xorg.conf file to handle dual monitors, using xinerama on my ati x1950pro, 3d acceleration has stopped working and I have been getting strange bugs (random X11 crashing when I try to open videos, etc etc).

Below is my xorg.conf. Can anyone spot any obvious mistakes?

```

# xorg.conf (X.Org X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier     "Default Layout"
	Screen      0  "Main Screen" 0 0
	Screen       1   "Second Screen" RightOf "Main Screen"
	Option	    "Xinerama" "true"
EndSection

Section "Files"
EndSection

Section "Module"
	Load  "dbe"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "Generic Keyboard"
	Driver      "kbd"
	Option	    "XkbRules" "xorg"
	Option	    "XkbModel" "pc105"
	Option	    "XkbLayout" "gb"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "Configured Mouse"
	Driver      "mouse"
	Option	    "CorePointer"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier   "Main Monitor"
	Option	    "VendorName" "ATI Proprietary Driver"
	Option	    "ModelName" "Generic Autodetecting Monitor"
	Option	    "DPMS" "true"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier   "Second Monitor"
	Option	    "VendorName" "ATI Proprietary Driver"
	Option	    "ModelName" "Generic Autodetecting Monitor"
	Option	    "DPMS" "true"
EndSection


Section "Device"
	Identifier  "aticonfig-Device[0]-0"
	Driver      "fglrx"
	BusID       "PCI:1:0:0"
	Option "DDCMode" "True"
	Option "MonitorLayout" "TMDS,TMDS" 
	Screen	0
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier  "aticonfig-Device[0]-1"
	Driver      "fglrx"
	BusID       "PCI:1:0:0"
	Option "DDCMode" "True"
	Option "MonitorLayout" "TMDS,TMDS"
	Screen 1
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier "Main Screen"
	Device     "aticonfig-Device[0]-0"
	Monitor    "Main Monitor"
	DefaultDepth     24
	SubSection "Display"
		Viewport   0 0
		Modes "1680x1050"
		Depth     24
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier "Second Screen"
	Device     "aticonfig-Device[0]-1"
	Monitor    "Second Monitor"
	DefaultDepth     24
	SubSection "Display"
		Viewport   0 0
		Modes "1280x1024"
		Depth     24
	EndSubSection
EndSection

```

If I run glxgears, the gears don't move. They did move before I added in dual monitor support.

### Post by wrtpeeps on 2008-07-19
bump?!

---

### Post by PmDematagoda on 2008-07-19
Execute:-
```
sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg
```
and restart the X-Server, then see if that fixes the problems a bit. 

Note:- Doing the command means reverting to either the ati or vesa driver which could result in no 3d acceleration, but you can reconfigure it to use fglrx again. Opening videos does not require 3D:).

---

### Post by philinux on 2008-07-19
Make sure you back up your xorg.conf file first.

---

### Post by Canis familiaris on 2008-07-19
> **philinux said:**
> Make sure you back up your xorg.conf file first.
BY:
```
sudo cp /etc/X11/xorg.conf /etc/X11/xorg.conf.bak
```
;)

### Post by Canis familiaris on 2008-07-19
I am no expert in configuring xorg.conf. But the lines I have marked in bold seem strange to me(scroll down to find the lines). I did not find similar lines in this Howto:
[https://help.ubuntu.com/community/XineramaHowTo](https://help.ubuntu.com/community/XineramaHowTo)

Perhaps try commenting them out by hash.

```


#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier     "Default Layout"
	Screen      0  "Main Screen" 0 0
	Screen       1   "Second Screen" RightOf "Main Screen"
	Option	    "Xinerama" "true"
EndSection

Section "Files"
EndSection

Section "Module"
	Load  "dbe"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "Generic Keyboard"
	Driver      "kbd"
	Option	    "XkbRules" "xorg"
	Option	    "XkbModel" "pc105"
	Option	    "XkbLayout" "gb"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "Configured Mouse"
	Driver      "mouse"
	Option	    "CorePointer"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier   "Main Monitor"
	Option	    "VendorName" "ATI Proprietary Driver"
	Option	    "ModelName" "Generic Autodetecting Monitor"
	Option	    "DPMS" "true"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier   "Second Monitor"
	Option	    "VendorName" "ATI Proprietary Driver"
	Option	    "ModelName" "Generic Autodetecting Monitor"
	Option	    "DPMS" "true"
EndSection


Section "Device"
	Identifier  "aticonfig-Device[0]-0"
	Driver      "fglrx"
	BusID       "PCI:1:0:0"
	Option "DDCMode" "True"
	Option "MonitorLayout" "TMDS,TMDS" 
	Screen	0
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier  "aticonfig-Device[0]-1"
	Driver      "fglrx"
	BusID       "PCI:1:0:0"
	Option "DDCMode" "True"
	Option "MonitorLayout" "TMDS,TMDS"
	Screen 1
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier "Main Screen"
	Device     "aticonfig-Device[0]-0"
	Monitor    "Main Monitor"
	DefaultDepth     24
	SubSection "Display"
		**Viewport   0 0**
		Modes "1680x1050"
		Depth     24
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier "Second Screen"
	Device     "aticonfig-Device[0]-1"
	Monitor    "Second Monitor"
	DefaultDepth     24
	SubSection "Display"
		**Viewport   0 0**
		Modes "1280x1024"
		Depth     24
	EndSubSection
EndSection

```

andy@pheonix:~$ glxgears
XIO:  fatal IO error 11 (Resource temporarily unavailable) on X server ":0.0"

### Post by markbuntu on 2008-07-19
You should really be using the Catalyst Control Center to configure your monitors.

---

### Post by wrtpeeps on 2008-07-19
> **markbuntu said:**
> You should really be using the Catalyst Control Center to configure your monitors.

How do I do this?

I type the command amdcccle and nothing happens. It just hangs there. (it doesnt give me an error about the command.)

---

### Post by markbuntu on 2008-07-20
OK. maybe you do not have it installed. You can get it with Synaptic. Just search amdcccle. You can then open it from terminal or find it in Applications/Other.

---
